data.title: Are U.S. Tariffs Working? China Hits Record $1.2 Trillion Trade Surplus. — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Reshma Kapadia

China's trade surplus widened to a record $1.2 trillion last year, even as the Trump administration targeted the world's second-largest economy with lofty tariffs to reduce such trade imbalances.

It raises the question of whether U.S. levies are making a dent on China's export dominance, and lessening this country's reliance on foreign goods. The answer is a nuanced one.

The U.S. collected about $200 billion in tariff revenue globally last year through mid-December, with the average effective tariff rate rising to 14% — from about 2.5% before President Donald Trump took office in January 2025 and reshaped trade policy. One major aim for the White House has been to reduce the U.S. trade deficit and punish countries whose exports far outpace their imports, like China.

There has been progress on the U.S. trade deficit, which narrowed to $29.4 billion in October 2025, its lowest monthly level since 2009.

"There's been a quantitative reduction in our dependence [on China], no question," says Derek Scissors, a China fellow at the American Enterprise Institute.

But more important than how much the U.S. is buying from China is what we are buying and that is still not clear, he adds. Final U.S. trade data for 2025 will offer a clearer picture of what the mix of imports has been. It will also provide a better gauge of how much of China's record trade surplus comes from transshipments — its exports to the rest of the world that ultimately work their way back into the U.S., often from Vietnam or Taiwan.

Full-year data are pending, but Scissors estimates that roughly 35% of the projected U.S. bilateral goods deficit decrease this year will be offset by such transshipments. As a result, the deficit may have narrowed by about $80 billion to $85 billion, rather than an estimated $125 billion, Scissors says.

Trade policy is still in flux. Trump has rolled back some tariffs on consumer-oriented goods as voters push back against rising costs. The Supreme Court is expected to weigh in on whether the administration overstepped its authority by using the International Emergency Economic Powers Act for a large spate of tariffs implemented last year. Trump this week said America is "screwed" if the Court rules those tariffs were implemented illegally. That uncertainty has undoubtedly affected trade flows.

Still, shipments from China to the U.S. fell 30% in December. Meanwhile, China's exports to Europe rose 12% and increased 13% to southeast Asian countries.

China's record trade surplus also reinforces Beijing's continued dependence on exporting its overcapacity to the rest of the world to steady its economic growth. That appears an easier task than the longer-term efforts needed to get its own consumers to buy more.

Until that changes, trade tensions are likely to persist.

Write to Reshma Kapadia at reshma.kapadia@barrons.com

BNB Info

BNB is the native coin that powers the BNB Chain ecosystem, including the BNB Beacon Chain and BNB Smart Chain. It serves several functions, such as reducing trading fees on the Binance exchange, paying for transaction fees on both the BNB Beacon Chain and BNB Smart Chain, and being utilized in games and DApps within the BNB Chain ecosystem. It’s also employed to participate in token sales hosted on the Binance Launchpad and provide liquidity on Binance Liquid Swap. Furthermore, BNB can be used for real-world applications, including online and in-store purchases and travel bookings on platforms like Travala.com.

As of September 2020, with the launch of the BNB Smart Chain, BNB can exist in three forms: BNB BEP-2 on the BNB Beacon Chain, BNB BEP-20 on the BNB Smart Chain, and BNB ERC-20 on the Ethereum network.

data.title: Visa crypto chief bets on stablecoin settlement, sees volumes growing
data.description:
Key points:
  • Visa says merchants don't accept stablecoin payments
  • Crypto chief highlights annualised $4.5 billion in stablecoin settlement
  • Visa sees demand for stablecoin-linked payment cards

By Elizabeth Howcroft

Visa is working to integrate stablecoins into existing payment systems, seeing an opportunity to maintain its market leadership as these tokens gain traction, Visa's head of crypto, Cuy Sheffield, told Reuters.

Stablecoins, cryptocurrencies typically pegged to the U.S. dollar, allow funds to be moved outside traditional banking systems. While their circulation has surged, led by El Salvador-based Tether's USDT with around $187 billion tokens in circulation, mainstream merchant acceptance remains limited.

Even if new payments systems are built using the technology behind stablecoins, "you still have to come back and connect to the existing merchant acceptance ecosystem if you want that product to be used," Sheffield said, referring to the current network of sellers that accepts payments.

NO CURRENT 'MERCHANT ACCEPTANCE AT SCALE'

Visa already has various stablecoin-related initiatives, including stablecoin-linked payment cards. In December it launched a pilot programme to allow some U.S. banks to settle with Visa using Circle's stablecoin, USDC.

Currently, there's no "merchant acceptance at scale" allowing holders to spend their stablecoins, Sheffield said.

This means that companies "need Visa's products and services more than ever to be able to actually get real customers using them," he said.

Visa's stablecoin settlement volumes have hit $4.5 billion in annualised run rate, a small fraction of the $14.2 trillion in annual payments volume Visa processed last year.

"But this is growing significantly month over month," Sheffield said. "We're seeing demand, and it's mostly this class of stablecoin-linked card providers."

SOME BANKS CONSIDER LAUNCHING THEIR OWN STABLECOINS

Some banks, including Goldman Sachs, UBS and Citi, last year said they were exploring launching their own stablecoin, following warnings that stablecoins could potentially undermine the role of commercial banks in global payment flows.

European banks, including ING and UniCredit, have formed a company to launch a euro-pegged stablecoin, to counter U.S. dominance in digital payments.

Visa's Sheffield said he was excited about euro-backed stablecoins.

"I think the stablecoin story shouldn't just be about dollars," he said.

HIGH-FREQUENCY TRADERS DOMINATE MARKET

There are more than $270 billion worth of stablecoins in circulation, more than twice the $120 billion from two years ago, according to a data website run by Visa and blockchain analysts Allium Labs.

Still, in a note last year, JPMorgan analysts said the idea of stablecoins replacing traditional money was far from reality.

Of $47 trillion in stablecoin transaction volume recorded on blockchain, Visa's website cited $10.4 trillion as "adjusted" volume.

Sheffield said the figure was revised down to remove volumes from high-frequency traders who arbitrage across different exchanges, as well as other non-payment activity.

data.title: Denmark to Ramp Up Military Presence in Greenland as U.S. Talks Conclude — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Anita Hamilton

Vice President JD Vance and Secretary of State Marco Rubio met with with Danish Foreign Minister Lars Løkke Rasmussen and Greenlandic Foreign Minister Vivian Motzfeldt at the White House to discuss the fate of Greenland.

The meeting lasted for about an hour. Shortly beforehand, both sides doubled down on their divergent positions regarding the future of the Danish territory.

The Danish Ministry of Defence said Wednesday that there would be an expanded military presence in and around Greenland "in close cooperation with NATO allies." It added that the purpose was to train and strengthen NATO's footprint in the Arctic region.

Additionally, Sweden's Prime Minister Ulf Kristersson said Wednesday that officers from the Swedish Armed Forces are arriving in Greenland today as part of a group of allied countries to prepare for "Operation Arctic Endurance," a military exercise to defend the region and conduct operations. "It is at Denmark's request that Sweden is sending personnel from the Armed Forces," he said in a post on X.

Denmark has said that the U.S. has " no legal basis" to annex Greenland, though President Donald Trump has argued that the U.S. needs the territory for national security reasons. The Arctic island's Prime Minister Jens-Frederik Nielsen said Tuesday, "If we have to choose between the USA and Denmark here and now, we choose Denmark."

Greenland's Prime Minister added that "the purpose is to train the ability to operate under the unique Arctic conditions and to strengthen the alliance's footprint in the Arctic, benefiting both European and transatlantic security."

This morning Trump reiterated his view that the U.S. must acquire Greenland. "It is vital for the Golden Dome that we are building," he posted on Truth Social early Wednesday.

The Golden Dome is a space-based missile defense system that the U.S. is looking to create. Strategically, Greenland is well-situated for national security purposes because the shortest route for a ballistic missile from Russia to the U.S. is over Greenland

The U.S. military base in Greenland, the Pituffik Space Base, was renamed in 2023 in part to reflect the traditional Greenlandic name for the area where the base is located. The U.S. Space Force is responsible for protecting satellites, missile warning systems and other space based operations.

Write to Anita Hamilton at anita.hamilton@barrons.com

data.title: Oracle sued by bondholders over losses tied to AI buildout
data.description:
Oracle was sued on Wednesday by bondholders who say they suffered losses because the company chaired by billionaire Larry Ellison failed to disclose it needed to sell significant additional debt to build out its artificial intelligence infrastructure.

The proposed class action was filed in a New York state court in Manhattan on behalf of investors who bought $18 billion of notes and bonds that Oracle issued on Sept. 25, two weeks after Oracle announced a $300 billion, five-year contract to supply OpenAI with computing power.

These investors said they were blindsided when Oracle returned to the capital markets seven weeks later to obtain $38 billion of loans to fund two data centers to support the OpenAI agreement .

"The bond market's reaction to Oracle's additional debt was swift and bracing," as prices fell and yields rose on Oracle's debt because investors perceived higher credit risk, the bondholders said.

According to the complaint, Oracle, Ellison, former Chief Executive Safra Catz, Chief Accounting Officer Maria Smith, and the company's underwriters are strictly liable under federal securities laws for false and misleading statements in offering documents for the $18 billion debt sale. The bondholders are seeking unspecified damages.

Oracle did not immediately respond to a request for comment.

data.title: Agi Files For Initial Public Offering
data.description:
By Elias Schisgall

Agi, the parent company of Brazilian bank Agibank, filed for an initial public offering.

The financial technology company did not disclose how many shares it would offer or at what price, according to its Wednesday filing with the Securities and Exchange Commission.

It intends to apply to list shares on the New York Stock Exchange under the symbol "AGBK."

Agi founder Marciano Testa said the IPO would grow the company's Smart Hub network and expand its footprint in areas of Brazil with limited access to the financial system.

The underwriters for the IPO are being led by Goldman Sachs, Morgan Stanley, and Citigroup Global Markets.

data.title: Trans American Aquaculture, Inc SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Trans American Aquaculture, Inc., a leading aquaculture company specializing in farm-raised Pacific white shrimp, has released its Form 10-Q report for the third quarter of 2025. The report provides a detailed overview of the company's financial performance and operational activities during the period, highlighting key metrics and future plans.

Financial Highlights

Total Revenues: $0. The company did not have shrimp for sale and only maintained broodstock for future genetic development and stocking.

Gross Profit: $0. No production, harvesting, or selling of shrimp occurred during the period.

Net Income (Loss): $(171,642). The net loss decreased compared to the same period in 2024 due to reduced interest expenses and lower general and administrative expenses.

Basic and Diluted Net Loss per Common Share: $(0.000095). Reflects the net loss per share for the period.

Business Highlights

Shrimp Inventory Management: The company maintains a broodstock inventory for future genetic development and restocking purposes. As of September 30, 2025, the inventory consists of 4,415 broodstock animals, which are crucial for the next production cycle planned for 2026.

Operational Challenges: The company did not produce or harvest shrimp during the reporting period, focusing instead on maintaining broodstock lines. This decision was influenced by the need for investment funds to refresh broodstock lines in the first quarter of 2026.

Business Overview: Trans American Aquaculture, Inc. is a leading aquaculture company specializing in farm-raised Pacific white shrimp, adhering to Best Aquaculture Practices guidelines. The company operates on a large-scale aquaculture farm in Rio Hondo, Texas, and aims to provide premium quality shrimp to the U.S. domestic seafood market.

Production and Harvest Cycles: The company typically engages in seasonal shrimp farming, with broodstock breeding occurring in the first quarter and grow-out processes spanning five to nine months. However, no stocking or harvest occurred in 2024 and 2025, with plans to resume in 2026.

Future Outlook: The company plans to raise additional capital to support the completion of developmental stage activities and ramp up full shrimp harvest cycles. The focus is on securing funding to operationalize current technology and establish a customer base.

Market Conditions: The shrimp industry has experienced a significant drop in prices, affecting the company's business prospects and financing capacity. The company is also facing competition from imported shrimp, which impacts commodity prices and customer demand.

SEC Filing: Trans American Aquaculture, Inc [ GRPS ] - 10-Q - Jan. 14, 2026

data.title: Cassation Court Date Set in 'First Arbitration' Proceedings
data.description:
Today EAM has learned that the Italian Court of Cassation has scheduled a datefor to hear EAM's appeal of the first arbitration award of 2019. EAM hadappealed to the Court of Appeal in 2019 and to the Supreme Court of Cassationin2021.

The Cassation Court has set the hearing date for 22 April 2026.

data.title: LiTHOS Announces Board and Management Change
data.description:
Vancouver, British Columbia--(Newsfile Corp. - January 14, 2026) - LiTHOS Group Ltd. (OTC Pink: LITSF) (FSE: YU8) (WKN: A3ES4Q) ("LiTHOS" or the "Company") announces the resignation of Jennie Choboter from the board of directors of LiTHOS (the "Board") and as chief financial officer of the Company. LiTHOS would like to thank Ms. Choboter for her contributions to the Company and wishes her the best of luck in her future endeavours.

Elyssia Patterson, of Vancouver, BC, has joined the Board following the resignation of Ms. Choboter. Ms. Patterson is CEO of Lycan Capital Corp., a corporate communications and marketing consulting company and has been a director and/or officer of several CSE listed companies.

ON BEHALF OF THE BOARD

Scott Taylor - CEO

For more information please contact:

1-877-LITHOS1 / 1-877-548-4671 or via email at info@lithostechnology.com.

To view the source version of this press release, please visit https://www.newsfilecorp.com/release/280392
